senses.definition: people or things that putt (in golf) or provide a putt in a golf game
examples: The club narrowed the lead with two excellent putters in the final round.
Several putters struggled on the green today.

senses.definition: small cherubic figures, often decorative, typically winged
examples: The fountain was decorated with delicate putti.
The Renaissance ceiling features many putti in playful poses.

senses.definition: a colloquial or dialectal term for a girl's bonnet or a small cap; can also refer to a simple plug or stopper in some dialects
examples: She wore a soft puttie on her head.
The old door had a leather puttie to stop the draft.

senses.definition: having been puttied; covered or sealed with putty
examples: The window frame was puttied before painting.
A puttied seam prevented any air leaks.

senses.definition: one who puts; a person who places or positions something; used in certain dialects or contexts
examples: The puttier arranged the bricks along the wall.
In old texts, a puttier often refers to a laborer who puts things in place.

senses.definition: people who put; plural form of puttier
examples: Several puttiers were employed to arrange the stones.
The hall was staffed by skilled puttiers in the old workshop.

senses.definition: a plural of putty; small portions of putty used for sealing or filling cracks
examples: He squeezed putties into the gaps between the tiles.
Tubular putties were used to stop the draft.

senses.definition: the act of placing something somewhere or executing a stroke in golf
examples: Putting is an important part of winning a golf match.
She spent extra time on putting practice.

senses.definition: to move something into a particular position or to perform the act of placing or performing a putt in golf
examples: He is putting the book back on the shelf.
She is puttingt-ing the ball on the green.
putting the ball

senses.definition: the act of putting or the things that are placed; generic plural of putting
examples: She tracked the puttings around the garden.
His notes covered the various puttings in the document.

senses.definition: a single cherubic child figure, often in art
examples: A marble putto supports the archway.
The painting features a playful putto with a garland.

senses.definition: a foolish or contemptible person; a slang insult
examples: Don't be a puttock and waste your money.
Only a puttock would say something so rude.

senses.definition: plural of puttock; fools or contemptible people collectively
examples: The crowd muttered at the actions of the puttocks.
They dismissed the critics as a bunch of puttocks.

senses.definition: a small or trivial thing; Danish dialect word for a toy or trinket; not widely used
examples: He bought a tiny puttoo for his nephew.
A puttoo sat on the shelf as a keepsake.

senses.definition: plural of putt; golf strokes made on the green to roll the ball into the hole
examples: He lined up three crucial putts on the final hole.
Her putting improved after weeks of practice.

senses.definition: soft, pliable material used for filling holes or sealing cracks; often oil-based or acrylic
examples: He used putty to seal the window frame.
The craftsman mixed putty to fill the gaps.

senses.definition: colored or stained with putty-like shade; pale brownish-pink
examples: She wore a putty-colored dress.
The walls had a soft putty tint.

senses.definition: a pipe or mechanism that expels putty; a tool used in construction
examples: The puttyblower helped smooth the sealant along the edge.
Repair workers used a puttyblower for the gap.

senses.definition: a person who uses putty; slang nickname for someone who works with sealants
examples: The crew joked that he was a real puttyhead.
In the shop, a puttyhead fixed the window frame.

senses.definition: soft-hearted or easily moved; made of putty in a figurative sense
examples: The puttyhearted child forgave quickly.
Her puttyhearted nature made her compassionate.

senses.definition: the act of applying putty to fill gaps or seal cracks
examples: He is puttying the cracks around the window.
Puttying is essential before painting.

senses.definition: lacking putty; not sealed or filled with putty
examples: The windows remained puttyless and drafty.
A puttyless surface was difficult to paint.

senses.definition: resembling putty in texture or consistency; soft and malleable
examples: The dough had a puttylike feel before it cured.
Her hands were puttylike after kneading for hours.

senses.definition: a plant species with root-like tubers; or a term in old texts for a putty-like root
examples: The gardener found a puttyroot specimen in the shade.
In herbal lore, puttyroot was valued for its thick root.

senses.definition: plural of puttyroot; the roots of that plant or historical term
examples: The field showed several puttyroots.
Old medicinal texts mention puttyroots among remedies.

senses.definition: work involving putty; patching, sealing, or cosmetic filling in construction or restoration
examples: The restoration included extensive puttywork on the window sashes.
They documented the puttywork before repainting.

senses.definition: a hypothetically derived form; unclear usage in contemporary English; may appear as nonce or error
examples: Puture is not a standard English word; verify the intended term.
